title: Brintooin

{{Quote|Brintooin itself is a world of plains, deserts, marshes, scrub, scattered woods, and other flatlands—ideal terrain for armored combat (the reason it was selected as the baseworld for the Hammers).|Special unit threat assessment report by New Republic Captain Zgorth'sth|<ref name="Journal15" />}}
'''Brintooin''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Seswenna sector/Legends|Seswenna sector]]. The planet's terrain included plains, [[Swamp/Legends|marshes]], and [[Desert/Legends|deserts]], as well as [[Plant/Legends|scrub]] and [[Forest/Legends|woodlands]]. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Battle of Turak IV]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], Brintooin was selected as the [[baseworld]] for the [[Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit/Legends|Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit]] and was populated by [[Human/Legends|Humans]] fiercely loyal to the Empire.

Following the heavy losses suffered by the Hammers during the Battle of Turak IV, the [[Vanguard armor regiment/Legends|regiment's]] [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Zel Johans]], arranged for the unit to be recalled to Brintooin for rebuilding. Johans organized extensive changes to his [[Armor regiment|regiment]], including an influx of new recruits and technological upgrades, all of which helped increase the size and strength of the unit and made Brintooin a center for [[Armor/Legends|armored]] warfare training.

When the Empire began to [[Imperial Civil War|splinter]] in the wake of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Johans eventually withdrew the Hammers and any affiliated forces to Brintooin, not wanting to participate in the fighting between the various Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]]. Over time, the colonel's forces on Brintooin became increasingly isolated from the larger [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflict due to the lack of efficient and independent [[Transport/Legends|transportation]] for their combat vehicles.

==Description==
Brintooin was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Journal15" /> located in the [[Brintooin system/Legends|Brintooin system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Seswenna sector/Legends|Seswenna sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Its terrain was dominated by various types of flatlands, including plains, [[Desert/Legends|deserts]], [[Swamp/Legends|marshes]], [[Plant/Legends|scrub]], and scattered [[Forest/Legends|woods]].<ref name="Journal15" />

==History==
===Base for the Hammers===
{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''[…]'' Johans was promoted to high colonel and the Hammers were recalled to Brintooin to train and build up to regimental strength.|Special unit threat assessment report by New Republic Captain Zgorth'sth|<ref name="Journal15" />}}
[[File:Hells_Hammers.svg|thumb|left|250px|Brintooin served as the baseworld for the Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit.]]
Since the conditions on Brintooin's surface were especially suited for [[Armor/Legends|armored]] combat, at some point before the [[Battle of Turak IV]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], the planet was selected as the [[baseworld]] for the [[Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit/Legends|Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit]], a [[Vanguard armor regiment/Legends|vanguard armor regiment]]<ref name="Journal15" /> of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]].<ref name="IS2">[[Imperial Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''Imperial Sourcebook'', Second Edition]]</ref> Following that engagement, which saw the Hammers reduced to roughly a third of its former size, the unit served at a [[Armor battalion|battalion]] level for more than two [[Standard year/Legends|years]], nonetheless experiencing a rapid succession of victories against the [[Alliance Military/Legends|Alliance forces]].<ref name="Journal15" />

Eventually, the Hammers' [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Zel Johans]], convinced the [[Imperial High Command/Legends|Imperial Command]] of the practical value of restoring his unit to [[Armor regiment|regimental]] strength,<ref name="IS2" /> and to that end, the Hammers were recalled to Brintooin for reorganization and retraining. Due to the unit's reputation, Brintooin experienced a massive influx of applications from across the Empire for transfer to the Hammers.<ref name="Journal15" />

On Brintooin, Johans, promoted to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[High Colonel/Legends|High Colonel]], orchestrated an intensive process of candidate selection, training of new recruits—as well as retraining of established [[Imperial Army trooper/Legends|troopers]]—and technological upgrades to the [[Regiment/Legends|regiment's]] equipment. The Hammers even trained specially selected [[Hovertank/Legends|tank]] crews transferred from other Imperial units, and Johans created three new regiments to serve in a proposed [[armored battlegroup]] along with the Hammers.<ref name="Journal15" />

===Imperial holdout===
{{Quote|Analysts believe Johans intended to wait out the fighting and join a side with a legitimate claim to inherit the Empire, as shown by circumstance. The units rested, repaired their tanks and other equipment and trained.|Special unit threat assessment report by New Republic Captain Zgorth'sth|<ref name="Journal15" />}}
[[File:Zel_Johans.jpg|thumb|right|200px|High Colonel Zel Johans made Brintooin into his power base.]]
Immediately following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="Journal15" /> of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> which saw the [[Death/Legends|death]] of the [[Palpatine/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] and the beginning of the Empire's [[Imperial Civil War|fragmentation]], the Hammers regiment and its affiliated units were shipped off Brintooin to participate in the ensuing Imperial crackdowns and battles between competing Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]]. When confronted with having to fight one of his own former units, Johans abandoned the faction the Hammers were fighting for and instead recalled the elite regiment and all affiliated units, as well as any willing Imperial [[Commander/Legends|commanders]], to withdraw to Brintooin.<ref name="Journal15" />

On Brintooin, the Hammers and its support regiments entered a period of rest, reparation of equipment, and additional training. Due to the lack of efficient, independent [[Star/Legends|interstellar]] [[Transport/Legends|transportation]] for the regiments' combat vehicles and Johans' discrimination regarding potential allies among other Imperial holdouts, the colonel's forces became increasingly isolated from the larger conflict between the Imperial remnants and the Rebel Alliance's successor, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="Journal15" />

===Reign of the New Republic===
{{Quote|Unless forced by circumstances to another course of action, Johans is likely to remain on Brintooin, isolated and untouchable.|Special unit threat assessment report by New Republic Captain Zgorth'sth|<ref name="Journal15" />}}
Johans did eventually ally with the [[Thrawn's confederation|forces]] commanded by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]; however, the latter's [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the New Republic ended too quickly for the Hammers to become involved in extended combat operations. Following the Grand [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral's]] [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|defeat]], Johans and the Hammers once again returned to isolation on Brintooin. By that [[Time/Legends|time]], the planet was administrated by Imperial [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Thalkuss]].<ref name="Journal15" />

[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zgorth'sth]] of the New Republic eventually compiled available information on the Hammers and Brintooin as part of a threat assessment report focusing on various special units. In the face of the formidable forces at Johans' disposal and the loyalty of Brintooin's population to the colonel, Zgorth'sth discouraged assorted [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic military]] leaders from attempting direct military actions against Brintooin, instead suggesting that a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] solution was in order.<ref name="Journal15" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|Life is somewhat restricted, but overall the population is loyal to the Empire as personified by Colonel Johans.|Special unit threat assessment report by New Republic Captain Zgorth'sth|<ref name="Journal15" />}}
[[File:1-H.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Many inhabitants of Brintooin were employed in the manufacture of repulsortanks.]]
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] population of Brintooin numbered 100 million and largely relied on the Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit for livelihood, which employed many in producing repulsortanks as well as in [[Engineer/Legends|engineering]] improved designs for the [[Tank/Legends|tanks]]. The planet was nominally administrated by an Imperial governor.<ref name="Journal15" />

Brintooin's inhabitants were fiercely loyal to Imperial rule, which was personified by the planetary hero and the Hammers' commanding [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]], Zel Johans, whose leadership closely related to the ideals propagated by the Imperial [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|Commission for the Preservation of the New Order]]. Captain Zgorth'sth of the New Republic characterized the life for the planet's population as being somewhat restricted but not under notable oppression.<ref name="Journal15" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|Brintooin Base became a center for armored warfare training, ''[…]''<nowiki></nowiki>|Special unit threat assessment report by New Republic Captain Zgorth'sth|<ref name="Journal15" />}}
Brintooin was the site of the military facility known as [[Brintooin Base]], which eventually came to serve as an Imperial center for armored warfare training. In addition, the Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit maintained an entire [[Factory/Legends|factory]] complex on Brintooin that was dedicated to the manufacture of repulsortanks. As estimated by [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]], the planet was also outfitted with formidable defenses, including [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shields]].<ref name="Journal15" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Zebitrope-Voyages13.png|thumb|right|250px|Map from ''Voyages SF'' 13 depicting Brintooin as part of the Zebitrope sector.]]
Brintooin was first mentioned in the [[1989]] ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'', written by [[Greg Gorden]] and published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Imperial Sourcebook">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] roleplaying game source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], placed the Brintooin [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Brintooin itself, in the [[Zebitrope sector]].<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref>

Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Brintooin system in grid square M-18,<ref name="TEA" /> and the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book further overrode the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement by establishing that the system was part of the Seswenna [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="EAOC" />
